Coming soon

Azlin Workspace

A native productivity suite — Office, Video, Calendar and a Drive alternative — each a real desktop app, not a browser tab. Fast, local-first, and powered by the open-source Azul rendering engine. No Electron, no telemetry, no subscriptions.

Get notifiedSee the UI toolkit →

Inside Azlin Workspace

One coherent suite of native desktop apps that share a single fast engine — so everything feels light, consistent and instant. Every app below is in development.

Office Coming soon

Documents, spreadsheets and presentations with native speed and open file formats.

Video Coming soon

Calls and meetings as a real desktop app — low latency, no browser tab eating your battery.

Calendar Coming soon

Scheduling that's yours: local-first, fast to navigate, and synced on your terms.

Drive Coming soon

A storage and file-sync alternative that keeps your data on hardware you control.

Mail Coming soon

A fast, native mail client that respects your inbox and never sells your attention.

…and more

Notes, contacts and the everyday tools a productivity suite needs — all sharing one engine.

What makes Azlin different

Native speed

Every Azlin app is a real native binary rendered by the Azul engine and Mozilla WebRender on the GPU — not a browser in a box. Instant startup, low memory, 60fps.

Local-first

Your files stay on your machine in open formats. No mandatory cloud account, no lock-in, and nothing phones home. The network is optional, not required.

No telemetry

No analytics SDKs, no ad trackers, no usage beacons. Azlin software is something you run, not something that watches you.

Open engine

Built on Azul, an MIT-licensed GUI framework with bindings for 11 languages. The toolkit under every Azlin app is free and auditable.

The Azlin family

Azlin Workspace Coming soon

The native productivity suite — Office, Video, Calendar, Drive and more. Learn more →

Azlin UI Available now

The open-source toolkit every Azlin app is built on. Open the docs →

Azlin OS Coming soon

A cohesive desktop where every app shares one engine. Learn more →

Frequently Asked Questions

Azlin is a family of native, privacy-respecting software built on the open-source Azul rendering engine. The flagship is Azlin Workspace — a productivity suite of native desktop apps (Office, Video, Calendar, a Drive alternative and more) — alongside the UI toolkit available today and a planned desktop OS. The goal is software that is fast, runs locally, and doesn't track you.

Not yet — Azlin Workspace and its apps (Office, Video, Calendar, Drive, Mail and more) are in development. What is available today is the Azul UI toolkit that the whole suite is built on. You can explore its documentation, examples and API right now under the UI section.

Azlin Workspace is planned as Office (documents, spreadsheets, presentations), Video (calls and meetings), Calendar, a Drive storage/sync alternative, Mail, and the everyday tools like notes and contacts — every one a real native desktop app sharing a single fast engine, available on desktop and mobile.

Azul is a cross-platform, MIT-licensed GUI framework that renders user interfaces on the GPU via Mozilla WebRender. It has bindings for 11 languages (Rust, C, C++, Python, and more) and is the rendering core under every Azlin app. Read the docs →

The engine targets Windows, macOS and Linux on the desktop, plus iOS and Android on mobile — all from a single codebase. Azlin apps inherit that same reach.

Electron ships an entire web browser with every app, which means hundreds of megabytes of RAM and slow startup. Azlin apps are compact native binaries that render directly on the GPU — a fraction of the memory, near-instant launch, and no bundled browser engine.

The Azul UI toolkit is fully open source under the MIT license, developed in the open on GitHub. The end-user Azlin apps build on that open core.

Azlin OS is a planned desktop operating environment where every application shares one fast rendering engine, so the whole system feels consistent and light. It's the long-term vision of the project and is not yet available.

© 2026 Azlin · Built on the open-source Azul engine · GitHub
